Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance inquiry" or "extension" (or similar) using search_emails to find the DOT extension email (dot_extension_granted) from linda.castellano@dot.gov granting a 15-calendar-day extension with revised deadline June 5, 2026 for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, and Marcus Thorne's forward to Chloe (marcus_fwd_extension_to_chloe) flagging that PHMSA requires signed originals or certified copies - unsigned drafts, internal memoranda, or verbal attestations will not be accepted - and asking whether the scanned PDF from Greg at Swift qualifies or whether the signed original needs to be overnighted.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "hazmat" or "DOT jurisdiction" or "audit" (or similar) using search_emails to find Chloe's initial audit email (chloe_reply_marcus_hazmat_audit) identifying four moves under DOT scope: (1) Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy - Houston to Portland, Class 9 lithium ~350 lbs UN3481, Swift, Apr 17-28, Airtable recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland; (2) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics - Columbus to Seattle, Epilog Fusion Pro 48 Class 3B CO2 laser 75W, Swift, Apr 15-23, Airtable recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle; (3) Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies - Chicago to Denver, Class 9 lithium UPS ~2,400 lbs, Swift, Apr 21-23, Airtable recRajPatelChicagoDenver; (4) Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack Energy - Phoenix to Seattle, 900 lb oversize rig, not hazmat but DOT-flagged for overweight/oversize permits, Heartland Movers, Apr 11-19, Airtable recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Swift" or "pricing" or "surcharge" or "rate increase" (or similar) using search_emails to find Greg Pallone's pricing update email (email_email_9a6a5e8dc032), subject "Pricing Update Effective Immediately - DOT Documentation Surcharge," announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new MoveOps bookings effective April 11 and a $500 per shipment DOT documentation surcharge where Swift crews handle added compliance paperwork. The email lists four in-progress shipments already incurring the $500 surcharge: Raj Patel (Dallas TX to Denver CO), Emilia Cruz (Phoenix AZ to Portland OR), Jordan Ekwueme (Cleveland OH to Columbus OH), and Nora Bennett (Indianapolis IN to Louisville KY).
OE 4: Search Slack with a query related to "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance" or "surcharge" or "Swift" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve conversation history from #operations (channel C006) and #finance (channel C005) using conversations_history, to find internal discussions including: Alejandro Fuentes' vendor exposure analysis referencing the Swift +15% and $500 DOT documentation surcharge; Chloe's status update confirming Greg's placarding cert for Keiko Tanaka (driver Mike Becerra, job #SR-2026-04-1538, 49 CFR 172.504); and Blessing Okafor's note that Swift drivers are spending ~45 minutes per shipment on new DOT compliance packets.
OE 5: Search or list Linear issues in the DOT compliance audit project using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or project: "project_dot_compliance_audit" or similar) to discover five tickets assigned to Chloe Vance: (1) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack - "DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium" - state: Done, all four checklist items verified; (2) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic - "DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser" - state: In Progress, gap identified: hazmat placarding certificate MISSING; (3) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind - "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" - state: Done, description claims all documentation verified; (4) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack - "DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig" - state: Done, not hazmat under 49 CFR 171-180, included for transparency; (5) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert - "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift - URGENT" - state: Todo, requesting signed cert from Greg Pallone.
OE 6: Read Linear comments on the DOT compliance issues using linear_list_comments (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) to discover: (a) comment_chloe_swift_confirmation on the Zhao issue (content actually pertains to Tanaka/Greg) - Greg confirmed April 22 that placarding was completed at transport, driver Mike Becerra affixed placards at Columbus pickup per 49 CFR 172.504, signed at loading and countersigned at Swift Seattle, job #SR-2026-04-1538, Greg sending scanned PDFs within 24 hours (EOD April 23) of the signed placarding certificate, Mosaic laser liability waiver, and Swift PHMSA registration #3281074 (valid through Sept 2027); (b) linear_comment_093d903c5cb9 on the Patel issue - flags that the issue is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located in the move packet, vendor email trail, or incident materials, and the Airtable record recRajPatelChicagoDenver still shows Status = In Progress despite the physical move being completed - a mismatch indicating the compliance ticket is stale and documentation is incomplete.
OE 7: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name/value related to hazmat or DOT or the specific employee names) or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to retrieve the four DOT-scoped relocation records and verify their current documentation status: (1) recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland - Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack, In Progress, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Class 9 lithium UN3481; (2) recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle - Keiko Tanaka, Mosaic, In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, Class 3B laser (cross-reference QuickBooks invoice INV-2026-0411 for $800 hazmat handling surcharge and $450 interim storage); (3) recRajPatelChicagoDenver - Raj Patel, NorthWind, In Progress, coordinator Blessing Okafor, Class 9 lithium UPS, chain-of-custody required; (4) recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle - Joaquín Reyes, GreenStack, In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, 900 lb oversize rig.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to Mosaic or NorthWind or hazmat) to find hazmat-related cost data: (a) inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (INV-2026-0411) - $9,010 total for Mosaic, includes Keiko Tanaka line items with $800 hazmat handling surcharge (laser, DOT placarding) and $450 interim storage (Swift Seattle Apr 15-23), both marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL; (b) inv_northwind_denver_02 (INV-2026-0412) - $14,700 total for NorthWind, includes Raj Patel $1,800 hazmat handling surcharge (Class 9 lithium UPS, DOT-certified) and $1,200 chain-of-custody documentation (marked PROVISIONAL estimate).
OE 9: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to DOT or PHMSA or Swift) to find: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) - $50,000 contingent liability accrual for estimated DOT/PHMSA civil penalties per Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, due date June 5, 2026, vendor U.S. DOT - PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division (vendor_dot_phmsa), approved by Marcus Thorne, citing the missing Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate and penalty exposure under 49 CFR 107.329.
OE 10: Look up Greg Pallone's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone" or similar) to find his email greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com and phone number before contacting him about whether the scanned certificate qualifies as a certified copy under PHMSA standards or whether the physical signed original must be overnighted.
OE 11: Look up coordinator contact information using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", "Javier Morales", "Blessing Okafor") to find their emails - fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, javier.morales@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com - for sending outstanding documentation notifications.
OE 12: Look up Marcus Thorne's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or similar) to find marcus.thorne@moveops.com before sending him the document inventory and cost exposure analysis.
OE 13: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to reflect actual documentation status for each DOT-scoped move - updating fields to indicate: Zhao has complete documentation (signed originals verified), Tanaka has scan only (placarding cert pending certified copy or signed original from Swift), Patel is missing signed custody log despite physical move completion, and Reyes documentation is complete (not hazmat, oversize permits on file).
OE 14: Update Linear compliance tickets using linear_update_issue to correct stale statuses: reopen issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (change state from Done back to In Progress or Todo) since the signed custody log is missing, and update issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert to reflect current status of Greg's scanned cert and whether physical original is needed. Add comments using linear_create_comment on the affected issues documenting the current documentation state and PHMSA's signed-originals-or-certified-copies requirement.
OE 15: Send an email from chloe.vance@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com using send_email requesting clarification on whether the scanned placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter shipment (job #SR-2026-04-1538) is a certified copy that meets PHMSA submission standards under Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, or whether the physical signed original needs to be sent via overnight courier to MoveOps. The email should also request any available documentation for the other Swift-handled DOT moves (Zhao, Patel) as signed originals or certified copies, given the June 5 deadline.
OE 16: Send an email from chloe.vance@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com using send_email with: (a) a complete document inventory for each of the four DOT-scoped moves showing what documentation is on file as signed originals, what exists as scans only, and what is missing - specifically flagging the Tanaka placarding cert (scan only, pending clarification with Swift on certified copy status) and the Patel custody log (missing, ticket was stale); and (b) a cost exposure analysis under Swift's new rates showing the +15% base rate increase on new bookings, the $500/shipment DOT documentation surcharge on in-progress compliance work, existing hazmat line items ($800 + $450 on Mosaic INV-2026-0411, $1,800 + $1,200 on NorthWind INV-2026-0412), and the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual.
OE 17: Send emails from chloe.vance@moveops.com to coordinators using send_email - to fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (re: Mei-Lin Zhao move), javier.morales@moveops.com (re: Keiko Tanaka and Joaquín Reyes moves), and blessing.okafor@moveops.com (re: Raj Patel move) - informing each coordinator what documentation is still outstanding on their assigned moves before the June 5 DOT deadline, and what actions are needed (e.g., Blessing needs to locate or obtain the signed custody log for Patel; Javier needs to confirm receipt of Swift's certified placarding cert for Tanaka). Alternatively, post a summary in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message covering all outstanding items and coordinator assignments.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance inquiry" or "extension" (or similar) using search_emails to find the DOT extension email (dot_extension_granted) from linda.castellano@dot.gov granting a 15-calendar-day extension with revised deadline June 5, 2026 for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, and Marcus Thorne's forward to Chloe (marcus_fwd_extension_to_chloe) flagging that PHMSA requires signed originals or certified copies - unsigned drafts, internal memoranda, or verbal attestations will not be accepted - and asking whether the scanned PDF from Greg at Swift qualifies or whether the signed original needs to be overnighted.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "hazmat" or "DOT jurisdiction" or "audit" (or similar) using search_emails to find Chloe's initial audit email (chloe_reply_marcus_hazmat_audit) identifying four moves under DOT scope: (1) Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy - Houston to Portland, Class 9 lithium ~350 lbs UN3481, Swift, Apr 17-28, Airtable recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland; (2) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics - Columbus to Seattle, Epilog Fusion Pro 48 Class 3B CO2 laser 75W, Swift, Apr 15-23, Airtable recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle; (3) Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies - Chicago to Denver, Class 9 lithium UPS ~2,400 lbs, Swift, Apr 21-23, Airtable recRajPatelChicagoDenver; (4) Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack Energy - Phoenix to Seattle, 900 lb oversize rig, not hazmat but DOT-flagged for overweight/oversize permits, Heartland Movers, Apr 11-19, Airtable recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Swift" or "pricing" or "surcharge" or "rate increase" (or similar) using search_emails to find Greg Pallone's pricing update email (email_email_9a6a5e8dc032), subject "Pricing Update Effective Immediately - DOT Documentation Surcharge," announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new MoveOps bookings effective April 11 and a $500 per shipment DOT documentation surcharge where Swift crews handle added compliance paperwork. The email lists four in-progress shipments already incurring the $500 surcharge: Raj Patel (Dallas TX to Denver CO), Emilia Cruz (Phoenix AZ to Portland OR), Jordan Ekwueme (Cleveland OH to Columbus OH), and Nora Bennett (Indianapolis IN to Louisville KY).
OE 4: Search Slack with a query related to "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance" or "surcharge" or "Swift" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve conversation history from #operations (channel C006) and #finance (channel C005) using conversations_history, to find internal discussions including: Alejandro Fuentes' vendor exposure analysis referencing the Swift +15% and $500 DOT documentation surcharge; Chloe's status update confirming Greg's placarding cert for Keiko Tanaka (driver Mike Becerra, job #SR-2026-04-1538, 49 CFR 172.504); and Blessing Okafor's note that Swift drivers are spending ~45 minutes per shipment on new DOT compliance packets.
OE 5: Search or list Linear issues in the DOT compliance audit project using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or project: "project_dot_compliance_audit" or similar) to discover five tickets assigned to Chloe Vance: (1) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack - "DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium" - state: Done, all four checklist items verified; (2) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic - "DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser" - state: In Progress, gap identified: hazmat placarding certificate MISSING; (3) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind - "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" - state: Done, description claims all documentation verified; (4) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack - "DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig" - state: Done, not hazmat under 49 CFR 171-180, included for transparency; (5) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert - "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift - URGENT" - state: Todo, requesting signed cert from Greg Pallone.
OE 6: Read Linear comments on the DOT compliance issues using linear_list_comments (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) to discover: (a) comment_chloe_swift_confirmation on the Zhao issue (content actually pertains to Tanaka/Greg) - Greg confirmed April 22 that placarding was completed at transport, driver Mike Becerra affixed placards at Columbus pickup per 49 CFR 172.504, signed at loading and countersigned at Swift Seattle, job #SR-2026-04-1538, Greg sending scanned PDFs within 24 hours (EOD April 23) of the signed placarding certificate, Mosaic laser liability waiver, and Swift PHMSA registration #3281074 (valid through Sept 2027); (b) linear_comment_093d903c5cb9 on the Patel issue - flags that the issue is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located in the move packet, vendor email trail, or incident materials, and the Airtable record recRajPatelChicagoDenver still shows Status = In Progress despite the physical move being completed - a mismatch indicating the compliance ticket is stale and documentation is incomplete.
OE 7: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name/value related to hazmat or DOT or the specific employee names) or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to retrieve the four DOT-scoped relocation records and verify their current documentation status: (1) recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland - Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack, In Progress, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Class 9 lithium UN3481; (2) recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle - Keiko Tanaka, Mosaic, In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, Class 3B laser (cross-reference QuickBooks invoice INV-2026-0411 for $800 hazmat handling surcharge and $450 interim storage); (3) recRajPatelChicagoDenver - Raj Patel, NorthWind, In Progress, coordinator Blessing Okafor, Class 9 lithium UPS, chain-of-custody required; (4) recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle - Joaquín Reyes, GreenStack, In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, 900 lb oversize rig.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to Mosaic or NorthWind or hazmat) to find hazmat-related cost data: (a) inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (INV-2026-0411) - $9,010 total for Mosaic, includes Keiko Tanaka line items with $800 hazmat handling surcharge (laser, DOT placarding) and $450 interim storage (Swift Seattle Apr 15-23), both marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL; (b) inv_northwind_denver_02 (INV-2026-0412) - $14,700 total for NorthWind, includes Raj Patel $1,800 hazmat handling surcharge (Class 9 lithium UPS, DOT-certified) and $1,200 chain-of-custody documentation (marked PROVISIONAL estimate).
OE 9: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to DOT or PHMSA or Swift) to find: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) - $50,000 contingent liability accrual for estimated DOT/PHMSA civil penalties per Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, due date June 5, 2026, vendor U.S. DOT - PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division (vendor_dot_phmsa), approved by Marcus Thorne, citing the missing Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate and penalty exposure under 49 CFR 107.329.
OE 10: Look up Greg Pallone's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone" or similar) to find his email greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com and phone number before contacting him about whether the scanned certificate qualifies as a certified copy under PHMSA standards or whether the physical signed original must be overnighted.
OE 11: Look up coordinator contact information using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", "Javier Morales", "Blessing Okafor") to find their emails - fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, javier.morales@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com - for sending outstanding documentation notifications.
OE 12: Look up Marcus Thorne's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or similar) to find marcus.thorne@moveops.com before sending him the document inventory and cost exposure analysis.
OE 13: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to reflect actual documentation status for each DOT-scoped move - updating fields to indicate: Zhao has complete documentation (signed originals verified), Tanaka has scan only (placarding cert pending certified copy or signed original from Swift), Patel is missing signed custody log despite physical move completion, and Reyes documentation is complete (not hazmat, oversize permits on file).
OE 14: Update Linear compliance tickets using linear_update_issue to correct stale statuses: reopen issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (change state from Done back to In Progress or Todo) since the signed custody log is missing, and update issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert to reflect current status of Greg's scanned cert and whether physical original is needed. Add comments using linear_create_comment on the affected issues documenting the current documentation state and PHMSA's signed-originals-or-certified-copies requirement.
OE 15: Send an email from chloe.vance@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com using send_email requesting clarification on whether the scanned placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter shipment (job #SR-2026-04-1538) is a certified copy that meets PHMSA submission standards under Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, or whether the physical signed original needs to be sent via overnight courier to MoveOps. The email should also request any available documentation for the other Swift-handled DOT moves (Zhao, Patel) as signed originals or certified copies, given the June 5 deadline.
OE 16: Send an email from chloe.vance@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com using send_email with: (a) a complete document inventory for each of the four DOT-scoped moves showing what documentation is on file as signed originals, what exists as scans only, and what is missing - specifically flagging the Tanaka placarding cert (scan only, pending clarification with Swift on certified copy status) and the Patel custody log (missing, ticket was stale); and (b) a cost exposure analysis under Swift's new rates showing the +15% base rate increase on new bookings, the $500/shipment DOT documentation surcharge on in-progress compliance work, existing hazmat line items ($800 + $450 on Mosaic INV-2026-0411, $1,800 + $1,200 on NorthWind INV-2026-0412), and the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual.
OE 17: Send emails from chloe.vance@moveops.com to coordinators using send_email - to fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (re: Mei-Lin Zhao move), javier.morales@moveops.com (re: Keiko Tanaka and Joaquín Reyes moves), and blessing.okafor@moveops.com (re: Raj Patel move) - informing each coordinator what documentation is still outstanding on their assigned moves before the June 5 DOT deadline, and what actions are needed (e.g., Blessing needs to locate or obtain the signed custody log for Patel; Javier needs to confirm receipt of Swift's certified placarding cert for Tanaka). Alternatively, post a summary in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message covering all outstanding items and coordinator assignments.

outcome

The email directly addresses whether the scan meets PHMSA standards (it doesn't) and requests physical originals.
✓Run #2The email to Greg explicitly references job #SR-2026-04-1538, the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment, and clearly states that PHMSA requires signed originals or certified copies and that scanned PDFs will NOT satisfy the submission requirements. ✓Run #4The email to Greg explicitly references Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, states scanned PDFs will not be accepted per PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 which requires 'signed originals or certified copies,' and requests physical originals via overnight courier.
✓Run #5The email to Greg explicitly states 'PHMSA will NOT accept scans' and requests certified hard copies specifically for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment, referencing Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538 and the signed cert from driver Mike Becerra on April 15.

5 The email to Marcus Thorne (visible in send_email or reply_to_email parameters) includes a document inventory that flags the Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies signed chain-of-custody log as missing, despite the corresponding Linear compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) having been marked Done.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✓✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1The agent never called linear_list_comments on the Patel ticket, so it missed the comment flagging the missing custody log. The email tells Marcus the log 'IS on file via the NorthWind supervised loading protocol' (fabricated based on the Linear issue description alone, which shows VERIFIED status). The email only asks Marcus to 'PLEASE VERIFY' as a caveat — it does not flag the custody log as actually missing. The rubric requires flagging it as missing despite the Done ticket.
✓Run #2The email to Marcus for Move 3 (Raj Patel) states: 'STATUS: COMPLETE — BUT ONE FLAG: I found that the Linear ticket was marked Done, but there's an open question from my earlier audit about whether the signed chain-of-custody log is physically attached or just referenced. Someone needs to physically verify the signed document is in our files.' This flags the chain-of-custody log issue despite the ticket being marked Done.
✗Run #3The agent never called linear_list_comments on the Patel ticket, so it missed the comment flagging the missing chain-of-custody log. The email to Marcus reports Raj Patel's documentation as 'COMPLETE — PHMSA-READY' with 'SIGNED ORIGINAL ON FILE.' The missing custody log was not discovered or reported.
✓Run #4The email to Marcus for Move 3 (Raj Patel/NorthWind) explicitly states 'the chain-of-custody ticket was marked Done but no signed custody log was actually attached anywhere' and flags it as 'NEEDS VERIFICATION.'
✗Run #5The agent never called linear_list_comments on issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, so it missed the comment flagging the missing signed chain-of-custody log. The email to Marcus reports Patel's documentation as 'COMPLETE for DOT purposes' with all 4 categories verified, which is incorrect.
✗Run #6The agent never called linear_list_comments for the Patel ticket and therefore never discovered the comment flagging the missing signed chain-of-custody log. 25 The model must pass a query or project parameter related to 'DOT' or 'compliance' or 'hazmat' or 'PHMSA' (or similar) when calling linear_list_issues, to find the DOT compliance audit tickets.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues with queries 'DOT compliance', 'compliance', 'hazmat', and 'DOT' — all related to DOT/compliance/hazmat terms. The 'DOT' query successfully returned the DOT compliance audit tickets in project_dot_compliance_audit.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues with query parameters 'DOT compliance', 'compliance', 'hazmat', and 'DOT' — all of which match the required terms (DOT, compliance, hazmat) for finding the DOT compliance audit tickets. The successful call with query 'DOT' and includeArchived:true returned all the relevant compliance issues.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'compliance' and also with query 'DOT' — both are directly relevant to finding DOT compliance audit tickets.
✓Run #4linear_list_issues was called with queries 'DOT compliance', 'hazmat documentation', 'compliance', 'PHMSA', 'DOT', and 'placarding' — all directly related to the DOT compliance audit context.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'DOT compliance' and then with query 'hazmat', both of which are relevant terms to find the DOT compliance audit tickets.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'DOT compliance', then again with query 'hazmat', and again with query 'DOT'. All three queries are relevant to finding DOT compliance audit tickets.
26 The model must pass 'issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind' (or a Patel/NorthWind-related issue identifier) as the issueId parameter when calling linear_list_comments, to discover the comment flagging that the signed chain-of-custody log is missing despite the ticket being marked Done.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called linear_list_comments at any point in the trajectory. It only read the issue descriptions via linear_list_issues, which showed the Patel ticket as 'Done' with all docs verified. The critical comment (linear_comment_093d903c5cb9) flagging the missing signed custody log was never retrieved, causing the agent to miss this key documentation gap.
✗Run #2The agent never called linear_list_comments at any point in the trajectory. It only called linear_list_issues and linear_update_issue. Without reading the comments on issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, the agent could not have properly discovered the comment flagging the missing signed chain-of-custody log. The agent's knowledge of this gap appears to have been hallucinated or derived from contextual inference rather than actual data retrieval.
✗Run #3The agent never called linear_list_comments at all in the entire trajectory. It never queried comments on 'issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind' or any other issue, which is why it missed the comment flagging the missing chain-of-custody log.
✗Run #4No linear_list_comments call was made in the trajectory. The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times but never called linear_list_comments with 'issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind' or any Patel-related issue ID to discover the comment flagging the missing signed chain-of-custody log.
✗Run #5The agent never called linear_list_comments at all in the trajectory. It updated issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind via linear_update_issue but never read the comments on that issue, missing the critical comment about the missing chain-of-custody log.
✗Run #6The agent never called linear_list_comments at all during the entire trajectory. No linear_list_comments tool call appears with issueId issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind or any other issue ID. This is why the agent missed the critical comment revealing the Patel chain-of-custody log is missing.
27 The model must pass a query or criteria parameter related to 'Mosaic' or 'NorthWind' or 'hazmat' or 'DOT' or 'PHMSA' or 'compliance' or 'penalty' (or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_bills, to find hazmat-related invoices and the DOT penalty accrual.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices with only {criteria: {limit: 50}} and quickbooks_search_bills with {fetchAll: true}. Neither call included any query or criteria parameter related to 'Mosaic', 'NorthWind', 'hazmat', 'DOT', 'PHMSA', 'compliance', or 'penalty'. The searches were generic with no hazmat/DOT-specific terms as required by this criterion.
✗Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria searching by vendor name 'Swift' — not any of the required terms (Mosaic, NorthWind, hazmat, DOT, PHMSA, compliance, penalty). The quickbooks_search_invoices was called with criteria: null (no filter). Neither search used terms that would surface the DOT penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) under vendor 'U.S. DOT - PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division', and the bills search for 'Swift' returned unrelated invoices (Kevin Tran, Priya Venkatesh, Ines Kovac moves) rather than the hazmat-specific invoices INV-2026-0411 or INV-2026-0412.
✗Run #3The quickbooks_search_invoices was called with null criteria (no relevant query terms), and quickbooks_search_bills was called with VendorRef.name LIKE 'Swift'. Neither query contains terms like 'Mosaic', 'NorthWind', 'hazmat', 'DOT', 'PHMSA', 'compliance', or 'penalty' — so the search would miss the DOT penalty accrual under the PHMSA vendor.
✗Run #4The QuickBooks calls used criteria that do not include DOT/hazmat/PHMSA/compliance/penalty/Mosaic/NorthWind terms: one was a generic search with just {limit: 50}, one used VendorRef LIKE 'Swift', and one used DocNumber 'INV-2026-0411'. None of these would surface the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) under vendor 'U.S. DOT - PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division'.
✗Run #5The quickbooks_search_bills calls used criteria of vendor_swift only (or fetchAll:true with no query), and quickbooks_search_invoices was called with an empty parameters object {}. None of these pass a query/criteria parameter specifically related to 'Mosaic', 'NorthWind', 'hazmat', 'DOT', 'PHMSA', 'compliance', or 'penalty' as required by this criterion.
✗Run #6The quickbooks_search_invoices call had no criteria/query parameters (returned all invoices generically). The quickbooks_search_bills call used criteria with VendorRef LIKE 'Swift'. Neither search passed terms like 'Mosaic', 'NorthWind', 'hazmat', 'DOT', 'PHMSA', 'compliance', or 'penalty'. The search for 'Swift' would find Swift vendor bills but miss the critical $50,000 DOT penalty accrual under vendor 'U.S. DOT - PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division'.
